title: Ghana calls on Commonwealth to condemn attack on its peacekeepers in Lebanon
content: Four soldiers were injured but all are now in a stable condition, Ghana's army says.

originalTitle: Guinea opposition leader urges 'direct resistance' after 40 parties dissolved
title: Speaking Up in Guinea: Why Different Ideas Matter!
content:
Hello from Your African News Friend!

Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you a story from our big, beautiful continent of Africa. Today, we're going to talk about a country called Guinea. It's a land full of wonderful people, green forests, and mighty rivers. Sometimes, in a country, people have different ideas about how to make things super good for everyone. It's like when you and your friends want to play different games – maybe one wants to play tag, and another wants to draw. It's okay to have different ideas, right?

Who is Mr. Cellou?

In Guinea, there is a kind man named Mr. Cellou Dalein Diallo. He is an important person because he leads a group of people who have ideas about how to help their country. He is like a captain of a team that wants to play a different game, but still wants to win for Guinea. Mr. Cellou and his friends want to make sure everyone in Guinea has a say in how their country is run. They believe that many different ideas can make a country stronger, just like having different colors in a rainbow makes it more beautiful. Did you know? Guinea is famous for its bauxite, which is used to make aluminum – the metal in your juice cans and some airplanes!

What are 'Parties' in a Country?

When we talk about 'parties' in a country, we don't mean birthday parties with cake and balloons! Here, a 'party' is a group of people who come together because they share similar ideas about how to make their country better. They all want to help. Imagine your class at school. Some kids might think having more art time is best, and they form an 'Art Ideas Group'. Other kids might think more sports time is best, and they form a 'Sports Ideas Group'. These groups are like mini-parties, all wanting to help the school.

What Happened to Many Groups?

Now, here's the part that made some people sad in Guinea. There were many different 'parties' or 'groups' with their own good ideas. But recently, a special leader in Guinea decided that many of these groups – like 40 of them! – were not allowed to be groups anymore. He told them they had to stop. Imagine if your teacher said, "Only the 'Art Ideas Group' can exist now! All other groups, like the 'Sports Ideas Group' or the 'Reading Ideas Group', must stop meeting." How would the kids in those groups feel? They might feel their voices are not being heard. Did you know? The capital city of Guinea is called Conakry! It's a busy city right by the ocean.

Who is the Country's Leader?

The leader who made this decision used to be a very important soldier, like a general in the army. Then, he became the main leader of the country, like a president. He is now in charge of everything in Guinea. Mr. Cellou Dalein Diallo believes that this leader wants to be the *only* one making all the big decisions. He thinks the leader doesn't want other groups to have different ideas or to try to help the country in their own ways. It's like wanting to be the only captain of every single team.

What Does 'Speaking Up' Mean?

Mr. Cellou said people should show 'direct resistance'. Now, this doesn't mean fighting or being mean. For kids like you, it means speaking up and showing you don't agree in a peaceful way. It means using your voice or your actions to say, "Hey, we have different ideas, and our ideas are important too!" It's like when you don't agree with a rule, you might raise your hand and politely ask, "Can we please try it this way?" or "I think this other idea might be good too." It's about making sure everyone gets a chance to share their thoughts and be heard, even when things are difficult. Did you know? The national animal of Guinea is the lion! Lions are strong and brave, and they live in groups called prides.

Why Are Different Ideas So Important?

Having many different groups and ideas is very important for a country. Think about a big puzzle. If you only have one kind of puzzle piece, you can't build the whole picture, right? You need all the different shapes and colors to make something beautiful and complete. In a country, different groups have different ideas about schools, hospitals, roads, and how to help people. When many voices are heard, it means the country can find the very best ideas to help *all* its people. It's about fairness and making sure everyone feels included and important.

Let's Keep Learning!

So, remember, kids: it's okay to have different ideas! In a family, in a school, and in a country, listening to everyone's ideas helps us find the best way forward. It helps us build a stronger, happier place for all. Keep learning about our amazing world, and always remember to use your voice for good! You are the future of Africa, and your ideas matter very much!
summary: An important leader in Guinea, Mr. Cellou Dalein Diallo, is unhappy because the country's main leader has stopped many groups with different ideas from existing, and Mr. Cellou wants people to peacefully speak up.
whyMatters: This matters because in a country, it's good when many people can share their ideas to help make things fair and find the best ways to help everyone, just like when friends share ideas to play a fun game together.

originalTitle: Bitter times for cocoa farmers as chocolate market slumps
title: Chocolate's Secret Heroes: Why Farmers Need Our Help!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, coming to you from beautiful Africa! Today, we are going to talk about something super yummy that almost everyone loves. Can you guess what it is? That's right, chocolate! Yum!

What is Chocolate Made From?

Did you know that chocolate doesn't just grow in a factory? Nope! It starts as a special bean called a cocoa bean. These beans grow inside big, colorful pods on trees. Imagine a big, bumpy fruit! Inside, there are lots of seeds, and those are the cocoa beans. These amazing cocoa trees love warm weather and lots of rain. That's why many of them grow right here in Africa, in places like Ghana and Ivory Coast. They grow in big, green forests. It's like a magical garden for chocolate!

Meet Our Chocolate Heroes: The Farmers!

So, who grows these special cocoa beans? They are very special people we call cocoa farmers! These farmers are like superheroes because they work super hard every single day to bring us the yummy chocolate we love. Most of these farmers live in West Africa. Their day starts very early, sometimes even before the sun wakes up! They walk through their farms, which are like big gardens filled with cocoa trees. They look after the trees and make sure they are healthy. It's a lot of work, just like taking care of a pet, but much bigger! Did you know? West Africa grows most of the world's cocoa! More than half of all the chocolate you eat starts its journey in countries like Ghana and Ivory Coast. Isn't that amazing?

A Day in the Life of a Cocoa Farmer

Imagine a farmer going to work. First, they check the cocoa pods on the trees. When the pods are big and ripe, they carefully cut them down. These pods can be yellow, orange, or even red! Each pod holds many beans. Then, they open the pods to get the beans out. It's a bit messy, like opening a big fruit! The beans are then dried in the sun. This makes them ready to travel to other countries. It takes many, many steps to get from a bean to a chocolate bar. This is very hard work. Farmers spend hours and hours outside, under the hot sun, making sure the cocoa beans are perfect. They do all this so we can enjoy a tasty chocolate treat.

A Big Problem for Our Heroes

But here is where our story gets a little sad, friends. Even though chocolate bars cost a lot of money in the shops, the farmers who grow the cocoa beans often get very, very little money for all their hard work. Imagine you helped your friend build a big, cool castle, and then your friend sold it for lots of toys, but only gave you one tiny toy. That's how it feels for many cocoa farmers. The price of chocolate in your shops might go up, but the money they get for their cocoa beans often stays super low, or even goes down. This means that even though chocolate is yummy and famous, the amazing people who grow it are finding it very hard to live.

What Happens When Farmers Don't Get Enough Money?

When farmers don't get enough money, it causes big problems for their families. They might not have enough money to buy food for everyone. It's hard to buy new clothes or even new shoes for their children. Sometimes, they can't even afford to send their kids to school. Imagine not being able to learn and play with friends at school because your parents don't make enough money from the cocoa beans they grow. It's a very difficult situation. Farmers work tirelessly, hoping their harvest will bring enough money to take care of their families. But when the price of cocoa beans is too low, all their dreams become very hard to reach. They are strong, but they need our help and understanding. Did you know? A cocoa farmer might only earn a few pennies for all the cocoa beans needed to make a big chocolate bar! It’s a tiny bit of the money we pay.

Why is This Happening?

You might wonder why this is happening. Well, sometimes there are many cocoa beans grown, more than what people need to buy right away. When there are too many beans, the people who buy them say, "We don't need to pay much because there are so many!" This makes the price go down. Also, there are big companies that buy cocoa beans from many different farms. Sometimes, they have so much power that farmers can't ask for a better price. Farmers have to sell their beans for whatever price is offered, even if it's not enough to live on. It's like when you have one toy, but many friends want to play, so you get to set the rules. But here, the farmers have many beans, and the buyers are fewer, so the buyers set the rules.

How Can We Help Our Chocolate Heroes?

So, what can we do, my wonderful friends? We can start by remembering the farmers. Every time we enjoy a piece of chocolate, let's think about the hands that grew the cocoa beans. We can ask grown-ups to choose chocolate from companies that promise to pay farmers a fair price. Some chocolate bars have a special sticker that says "Fairtrade" or "Ethical Sourcing." This means the company tries to give farmers a better deal. It's like buying a toy that you know helped the person who made it. By choosing these chocolates, we are sending a message that we care about our chocolate heroes, the cocoa farmers of Africa. We want them to get enough money for their hard work, so their families can be happy and healthy, and their children can go to school.

Let's Remember Our Farmers!

Thank you for listening, everyone! It's so important to know where our food comes from and to care about the people who grow it. Let's send a big cheer to all the cocoa farmers in Africa! You are truly our chocolate heroes! Keep being kind and caring, and I'll see you next time with more news from Africa! Bye for now!
summary: Cocoa farmers in West Africa work hard to grow beans for our chocolate but get very little money, making it hard for their families, so we should try to choose chocolate that helps them.
whyMatters: It matters because these farmers are real people, and if they don't get enough money, they can't buy food or send their children to school, which means their lives are very tough, even though they help make something yummy for us.

originalTitle: Most victims of US-Israeli attacks on Iran are civilians
title: Most victims of US-Israeli attacks on Iran are civilians
content: Iran’s deputy health minister, Dr Ali Jafarian, says more than 1,255 people have been killed and that most are civilians

originalTitle: Iranians celebrate as Mojtaba Khamenei named new supreme leader
title: Big News from Far Away: Iran Has a New Big Helper!
content:
Jambo, my amazing young friends! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share exciting stories from all over our wonderful world. Today, we have some special news coming from a country very far away called Iran.

Hello to a New Helper!

Imagine you have a big team at school, and you need someone super important to help everyone make good choices and keep things running smoothly. Well, in the country of Iran, they just got a new special helper! His name is Mojtaba Khamenei. He has a very important job, like being the head of a very big family – the whole country! People in Iran are very happy and they gathered together to celebrate this big news.

What is a 'Supreme Leader'?

This special job is called 'Supreme Leader'. It means he is like the most important person in the whole country. He helps make big decisions for all the people living in Iran. Think of it like this: your parents or guardians make important rules for your home to keep you safe and happy. A Supreme Leader does that for an entire country! It's a very big responsibility. Did you know? Different countries have different kinds of leaders! Some have presidents, some have kings or queens, and some have a Supreme Leader. They all work to help their people!

Where is Iran?

Iran is a country far, far away from us here in Africa. It's in a part of the world called Asia. You might see it on a big map if you look closely! Even though it's far, we can still learn about the people there and what is happening. The world is like a very big neighbourhood, and we are all neighbours! Iran is a country with lots of history and beautiful places. It has mountains, deserts, and even some green areas. Did you know? Iran is famous for its beautiful handmade carpets! They are so colourful and have amazing patterns. People work very hard to make them.

Why Are People Celebrating?

When someone new gets a very big and important job, people sometimes celebrate! It's like when you have a birthday party, or when your favourite sports team wins a big game. People in Iran are happy because they believe Mojtaba Khamenei will do a good job leading their country. They hope he will make good decisions that help everyone live better lives. They gathered in different places, talking and sharing their happiness. Celebrations often mean getting together with friends and family.

Who is Mojtaba Khamenei?

Mojtaba Khamenei is a grown-up man who has been learning and preparing for a very long time to help lead Iran. He is like an older brother or a wise elder in a big family. He has a lot of experience and knowledge that people believe will help him guide the country. It's like when a big brother knows a lot because he has been around longer!

What Does a Leader Do?

A leader's job is to think about all the people in the country. They make sure there are schools for children like you to learn. They help make sure there are hospitals for when people get sick. And they work to make sure everyone has food to eat and a safe place to live. It's a very busy job, full of important meetings and decisions every single day. They have to listen to many people and think carefully. Did you know? Leaders also help countries be friends with other countries around the world! It's like making new friends at school, but on a much bigger scale!

Learning About Our World

Isn't it amazing to learn about different countries and people? Even though Iran is far away, it's good to know what's happening there. It helps us understand that we are all part of one big global family. And everyone, everywhere, wants to live in peace and have good leaders. So, remember this big news from Iran! A new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is starting his very important job to help his country. We wish him and the people of Iran all the best! That's all the news for today, my little explorers! Keep curious and keep learning about our amazing world. See you next time!
summary: People in the country of Iran are celebrating because Mojtaba Khamenei has been chosen as their new special leader, called the Supreme Leader, who will help make big decisions for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because the Supreme Leader is like the most important helper for all the people in Iran, making big rules and choices that affect their daily lives, like going to school or having safe homes.
